summary: Porta discusses methods for detecting hidden messages in letters, emphasizing observation of letter frequency, position, and context to decipher ciphers.

entities.name: cipher_method
entities.description: Permutation of characters by occultation (z.caps.6) as a method for revealing hidden messages
entities.page_ref: Book X Ch Y or null

entities.name: material
entities.description: Letters used in ciphers, divided and numbered (up to 25 total)
entities.quote: there are not more than twenty-one, or rather two and twenty-five in total

entities.name: concept
entities.description: Hidden messages in letters revealed through frequency analysis and positional clues
entities.quote: Sermo enim in epistola contentus aliquando frequentissimas ratissimas, & rariissimas frequentissimas faciet

entities.name: device
entities.description: Use of directional methods (right hand) and positional markers (end, middle, fertile line)
entities.quote: in whatever way, or in any order, or by any method of the right hand, or in the end, or in the middle

entities.name: concept
entities.description: Division of letters into groups based on frequency and position for cipher analysis
entities.quote: the division of this kind in the Scripta Prætoria is « 4

entities.name: concept
entities.description: Use of vowel and consonant analysis to determine cipher structure
entities.quote: they can be pronounced with vowels and consonants, as indicated

entities.name: location
entities.description: Scripta Prætoria (context for letter division method)
entities.quote: the division of this kind in the Scripta Prætoria is « 4

entities.name: historical_example
entities.description: Not explicitly stated; references to general cipher use without specific historical case

entities.name: concept
entities.description: Ciphers should be understood through proportion and order, not chance
entities.quote: the true nature of the word is known: which when clearly tested will be produced by the right proportion and order
